banner: DRAFT — NOT SUBMITTED — review with a human before proceeding
scenario: Illustrative mock applicant: Marcus Devon Reyes, requesting his own Florida birth certificate by mail. He is the registrant himself, so he is directly eligible under Section 382.025 and no attorney or deceased-registrant sub-flow applies. He knows his exact date of birth, so the unknown-year search fee does not apply. He requests one computer-generated certification, no rush service, paid by check. All names, numbers, and identifiers are fabricated test data, not a real applicant's information.

eligibilityOutcome.summary: Directly eligible as the registrant named on the birth record (of legal age).

reasons.requirement: Florida law (Section 382.025) limits who may receive a birth certificate less than 125 years old. Selecting a category outside the five directly-eligible ones routes the applicant to the notarized DH 1958 affidavit path instead of ending the request.
reasons.sourceRef: DH 726 reverse side, 'ELIGIBILITY (Section 382.025, Florida Statutes)' — numbered list 1-6

collectedValues.label: Which of these describes you, the applicant?
collectedValues.sourceRef: DH 726 reverse side, 'ELIGIBILITY (Section 382.025, Florida Statutes)' — numbered list 1-6

collectedValues.label: Are you requesting this record as an attorney representing a client?
collectedValues.sourceRef: DH 726 — 'If applicant is an attorney, provide name of client you represent...'

collectedValues.label: Mother's / parent's full name prior to first marriage
collectedValues.sourceRef: DH 726 — "Mother's / Parent's Full Name Prior to First Marriage: ___"

collectedValues.label: Father's / parent's full name prior to first marriage
collectedValues.sourceRef: DH 726 — "Father's / Parent's Full Name Prior to First Marriage: ___"

collectedValues.label: Type of certificate
collectedValues.sourceRef: DH 726 — 'Computer-Generated Certification of Birth' / 'Photocopy-Generated Certificate of Live Birth'

collectedValues.label: Rush order
collectedValues.sourceRef: DH 726 — 'Rush Order (Optional): Rush orders are given priority processing and requires an additional $10 fee.'

collectedValues.label: Payment method
collectedValues.sourceRef: DH 726 reverse side — 'FEES: Check or Money Order Payable to: Vital Statistics. DO NOT SEND CASH.'

fieldsNotApplicable.label: Your relationship to the deceased registrant
fieldsNotApplicable.sourceRef: DH 726 reverse side — 'a birth certificate marked "Deceased" may be issued to registrant's spouse, child, grandchild, sibling, if of legal age (18)'

fieldsNotApplicable.label: Name of client you represent
fieldsNotApplicable.reason: isAttorneyRequestor is false; the applicant is not an attorney.
fieldsNotApplicable.sourceRef: DH 726 — 'provide name of client you represent: ___'

fieldsNotApplicable.label: Client's relationship to registrant
fieldsNotApplicable.reason: isAttorneyRequestor is false; the applicant is not an attorney.

fieldsNotApplicable.label: Bar / professional license number
fieldsNotApplicable.reason: isAttorneyRequestor is false; the applicant is not an attorney.

fieldsNotApplicable.label: Additional years to search (if exact birth year is unknown)
fieldsNotApplicable.reason: The applicant knows the exact date of birth; the unknown-year search fee does not apply.
fieldsNotApplicable.sourceRef: DH 726 — 'Additional Years Searched: $2.00 per year (Required when exact year is unknown. Maximum fee is $50.00)'

documents.label: Applicant's valid photo identification
documents.note: A copy of the applicant's Florida driver's license was included, front and back.

documents.label: Legal guardianship papers
documents.sourceRef: DH 726 reverse side — 'Legal Guardian (must provide guardianship papers)'

documents.label: Documentation of legal representative status
documents.sourceRef: DH 726 reverse side — 'Legal representative of one of the above persons (must provide documentation)'

documents.label: Certified copy of court order
documents.sourceRef: DH 726 reverse side — 'Other person(s) by court order (must provide certified copy of court order)'

documents.label: Photocopy of registrant's death certificate
documents.note: requestorEligibilityCategory is 'registrant'; the registrant is the living applicant.
documents.sourceRef: DH 726 reverse side — 'include a photocopy of the death certificate'

documents.label: Notarized DH 1958 Affidavit to Release a Birth Certificate
documents.note: requestorEligibilityCategory is 'registrant', a directly-eligible category; the affidavit is only required for 'other_not_directly_eligible'.
documents.sourceRef: DH 726 reverse side — 'the application must be accompanied with a notarized Affidavit to Release a Birth Certificate form (DH 1958)'

documents.label: Photo ID of the eligible person authorizing release
documents.note: requestorEligibilityCategory is 'registrant', a directly-eligible category; no separate authorizing person applies.
documents.sourceRef: DH 726 reverse side — 'a copy of valid photo identification of both, the person authorizing release and the applicant'

documents.label: Fee payment
documents.note: A personal check for $10.00 ($9.00 first computer certification + $1.00 shipping and handling) made payable to 'Vital Statistics' was enclosed.
documents.sourceRef: DH 726 reverse side — 'FEES: Check or Money Order Payable to: Vital Statistics. DO NOT SEND CASH.'

documents.label: False-statement penalty acknowledgment and signature
documents.note: The applicant signed the application, acknowledging the felony false-statement warning.
documents.sourceRef: DH 726 — statute warning printed above the applicant fields, paired with 'Signature: ___'

safetyNotice: This packet was assembled by an automated agent from a GovSchema document (us/fl/doh/birth-certificate-request). GovSchema describes and verifies government processes; it does not submit them. No value in this packet has been transmitted to the Florida Department of Health or any government system. A human (the applicant) must review every value below, confirm it is correct, sign the physical application, enclose the required identification and payment, and mail or hand-deliver it to the Bureau of Vital Statistics before it has any effect. This packet is not a government record and carries no legal effect on its own.
